entry.title: Re: Business transaction "unlock" in SD status profile do...
entry.content:
What you are running into is a limitation of how user status profiles and business transactions are linked in SD. The “Unlock” (BUNL) business transaction in status management does not correspond to “clearing an invoice block” in sales order processing. In other words, the action you are testing (removing a billing block) is not technically mapped to the BUNL transaction in the status profile.

In status management, each business transaction code (like BUNL, BUCH, etc.) is tied to specific system functions. BUNL = Unlock is generally used in PS/PM contexts (unlocking objects), not for SD billing block removal. That is why your user status is not being set when you clear the invoice block.

Below are some options to achieve your requirement

1) Customizing in Status Profile
Adjust the profile so that the correct business transaction (e.g., “Release for billing”) sets the user status.

2) User Exit / BAdI
If no standard business transaction exists for “invoice block cleared,” you can implement a user exit (e.g., USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT in MV45AFZZ) or a BAdI to set the user status programmatically when the billing block field changes from blocked → blank.

3) Workflow Alternative
Another option is to trigger a workflow/event when the billing block is removed, which then updates the user status.

entry.title: Re: Process order settlement error
entry.content:
Hello Padalava,

It seems that you've implemented 3428636 - KKS9: Price difference split enhancements

Then, based on this Note,

C) Adjustment: Any difference is posted to the adjustment account defined here. This prevents the termination of processing as in case a) and enables the subsequent display and possible correction of such differences. If such a standardizing entry is generated while processing the different currency types, this is also taken into account for all postings of this sender according to method b).
Since the system may automatically change the method to c), you should always define an account here to prevent the corresponding error message FCO_SFIN 170.

-> Please follow the hyperlink in the long text of the message and add the account you need in the customizing in order to prevent the error FCO_SFIN170.

Hope this help.

SAP Profitability and Cost Management

entry.title: Re: Quantum Computing Meets the Classroom: SAP University Alliances participates to Quantum Seminar
entry.content:
Great initiative by SAP University Alliances, IBM, and DHBW Stuttgart. Using hands-on demos like RasQberry and the LEGO IBM Quantum System Two model is a smart way to make complex quantum computing concepts more accessible and engaging for students.

It’s exciting to see SAP exploring how quantum concepts can connect with SAP BTP and enterprise applications. Bridging academic learning with future business technologies will definitely help prepare students for the next generation of enterprise computing.

entry.title: Re: Is it Possible To Create Work Flow For Business Partner Create/Change In SAP S/4 Cloud Public Ed
entry.content: As of now, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not provide a standard flexible workflow for Business Partner creation or change similar to workflows available for Sales Orders, Purchase Orders, pricing, etc. A possible workaround is to use SAP Build Process Automation or SAP BTP Workflow to create an approval process around BP creation/change requests. The process can capture the request, route it for approval, and then trigger BP creation/update through available APIs or integration services. Another approach some customers use is restricting direct BP maintenance authorizations and managing requests through custom apps or forms with approval logic outside the core standard workflow. Would be interested to know if SAP plans to deliver a standard BP workflow in future releases.

entry.title: Group Reporting - Two Tenants with different FYV (4-4-5 and K4)
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

A customer operates only in the US and has two different lines of business: Retail and Real Estate.

The customer initially requested to use Fiscal Year Variant K4 in the leading ledger and SZ (4-4-5) in the non-leading ledger. However, we understand that this scenario is currently not supported because industry-specific fiscal year variants cannot be used as alternative fiscal year variants.

The customer understands this restriction and would like to know if it would be possible to implement two separate systems - one for the companies using FYV K4 and another for those using FYV SZ.

My questions are:

  • In relation to Group Reporting functionality, is there a way to map or consolidate two different fiscal year variants that do not share the same start and end dates?

entry.title: Credit Management for Professional Services Project Billing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, specifically with Professional Services / Customer Projects and the Project Billing process.

Our business requirement is to use Credit Management to prevent project billing when a customer has an outstanding or overdue balance above a certain threshold.

For example:

Scenario 1:
A customer has overdue open items greater than EUR 20,000. When the billing specialist tries to create or process a Project Billing Request in Manage Project Billing, the system should prevent the billing from moving forward.

Scenario 2:
A customer has exceeded their credit limit. Before generating the Billing Document Request or final invoice for a customer project, the system should either block the process or require approval from Credit/Finance.

I found the configuration activity “Activate Credit Management in Service Transactions”. However, these seem to be related to Service Management objects, not directly to Professional Services Customer Projects or Project Billing Requests.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/adbae5bcd5994f159bf2847a11397b61/2f7ce9927b424492b5e3e25656e8bfca.html?locale=en-US

My questions are:

Is there any standard way in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to activate Credit Management checks for Professional Services Project Billing?
Can Credit Management automatically block the creation of a Project Billing Request, Billing Document Request, or invoice when the customer exceeds a credit limit or overdue amount?

Any guidance, documentation reference, or experience from similar implementations would be very helpful.

Thank you in advance.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: debugging software that connects to SAP ASE
entry.content:
The trace appears to be in the ODBC driver, not ASE, IMHO.  I would suggest opening a support incident with SAP instead of attempting to fix yourself.  Possible issues can then be tracked and resolved through a case, especially if it touches other components of the product, or requires engineering support.

There is also a possibility that any issue could be known and already being worked on or fixed.

Chris

entry.title: How we can pass parameters from UI5 application to HTML for gui app in fiori lauchpad?
entry.content:
Here is my custom application where sales order is generated with billing document number and parcel number. Those Billing document and parcel number i have to display in HTML for GUI app (Receipt Voucher) custom ABAP report in Fiori Launchpad. 

  Above billing document number and parcel number i have to pass to HTML for GUI app (Receipt Voucher)

Here is the js function i have written 

onReceiptBoucher: function() {
var sBillingDoc = 78454656565;
var sParcelNo = 5454626526;
 
if (!sBillingDoc || !sParcelNo) {
sap.m.MessageBox.error("Please generate billing document first.");
return;
}
 
var xmservice = sap.ushell && sap.ushell.Container &&
sap.ushell.Container.getService("CrossApplicationNavigation");
 
if (xmservice) {
var hash = xmservice.hrefForExternal({
target: {
semanticObject: "ZRV",
action: "display"
},
// Parameters perfectly match your configuration mapping
params: {
"BillingDoc": [String(sBillingDoc)],
"ParcelNum": [String(sParcelNo)],
"DYNP_OKCODE": ["=ONLI"] // Bypasses selection screen by clicking Execute
}
});
 
var url = window.location.href.split('#')[0] + hash;
sap.m.URLHelper.redirect(url, true);
} else {
sap.m.MessageToast.show("Fiori Launchpad services are unavailable.");
}
}

entry.title: Anyone using AI agents with SAP OData in production — what’s breaking the most right now?
entry.content:
Genuine question for anyone running SAP + AI agents in production or POC:

I've been trying to figure out the actual technical failure modes of LLM agents on SAP OData — separate from the loud Policy v4/2026 debate.

In my own testing on the ES5 sandbox, even simple business questions like "find all open sales orders for a customer" produced wrong OData URLs more often than I expected. Hallucinated field names (SalesOrderID instead of SalesOrder), wrong navigation paths, unsupported $filter syntax.

A few things I'd genuinely value input on:

1. If you've built something that works reliably against real S/4HANA (not sandbox) — what made the difference? Knowledge
graphs, fine-tuning, semantic layer, something else?

2. What's the failure mode you keep hitting that nobody's written about? The quiet ones.

3. For European customers under DSAG's flag — how are you actually thinking about Policy v4/2026 architecturally? Are you waiting, pivoting, or building something compliant?

 Trying to map the real terrain before committing more weekends to it.

entry.title: Re: Open Item Management have been determined accordingly...
entry.content:
Hello @leah_huang71 ,

Your issue occurs because SAP derives Open Item Management and related posting behavior before the user exit replaces the G/L account. Directly removing BSEG-XOPVW = 'X' is risky because it can negatively impact GR/IR clearing and MM-FI reconciliation processes. The safer approach is to align the replacement account settings with the original account or enhance the account determination earlier in the process so SAP derives all related attributes consistently from the final account itself.

 

Issue / QuestionExplanation & Recommendation
Current situationStandard OBYC determines GR/IR account as 4* account. Your user exit successfully replaces it with 2* account during GR/IR posting.
Problem observedAlthough FI posting uses the replaced 2* account, SAP still derives: ✔ Open Item Management (OIM) ✔ Assignment behavior based on the original 4* account determination logic.
Why this happensSAP determines several account-control attributes (OIM, assignment, field status, reconciliation logic) earlier in processing before your substitution/user exit changes the final G/L account.
Important pointReplacing only the account number in a user exit does NOT fully re-trigger all standard account attribute derivations.
About VWTYGB01-BSEG-XOPVWField BSEG-XOPVW controls Open Item Management behavior at document line level.
Risk of removing X from BSEG-XOPVW⚠ High risk. Removing it manually/enhancing logic can affect: ✔ GR/IR clearing ✔ Automatic clearing ✔ MM-FI reconciliation ✔ Reporting consistency ✔ GR/IR aging and clearing processes
Why SAP sets XOPVWSAP sets this based on original account determination and account master settings to ensure GR/IR behaves correctly for logistics integration.
Technical concernFor MM-integrated postings, SAP tightly controls OIM behavior because GR/IR is a core reconciliation process between MM and FI.
Recommended approach✔ Best practice is to solve account determination in OBYC/customizing if possible rather than replacing accounts later via user exit.
If enhancement is unavoidableEnsure the replacement account (2*) has identical settings to original account (4*): ✔ OIM ✔ Field status ✔ Sort key ✔ Currency settings
Better enhancement approachInstead of manipulating XOPVW, enhance logic earlier in account determination process so SAP derives all dependent attributes consistently from the final account.
What NOT recommended❌ Directly forcing/removing XOPVW without full regression testing.
Areas requiring regression testing✔ GR/IR clearing ✔ MR11 ✔ F.13 automatic clearing ✔ Logistics invoice verification ✔ Reporting


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: [Bug] in method cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds
entry.content:
There was a question in 2023 alleging a possible bug, proposing solutions, but there was no answer: [Bug] in method cl_cds_test_environment=>create_fo... - SAP Community.

I'm posting it again as the original question is now "read only".

That question was saying that the below code fails at line 3, when mixing CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT=>CREATE_FOR_MULTIPLE_CDS and CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT=>CREATE:

cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds( 
             i_for_entities = VALUE #( ( i_for_entity = 'I_PRODUCT' ) ) ).
cl_osql_test_environment=>create( 
             i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'MARA' ) ) ).

That question said there was no bug if using CREATE instead of CREATE_FOR_MULTIPLE_CDS of the class CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT:

cl_cds_test_environment=>create( i_for_entity = 'I_PRODUCT' ).
cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'MARA' ) ) ).

That question was proposing 2 solutions to mock multiple CDS views:

  1. One targeted to SAP,  by inserting the test "IF test_associations EQ 'X' " in CREATE_MUL_CDS_TEST_INSTANCE, the same way it's done in CREATE_CDS_TEST_INSTANCE (NB: the question did not mention the ABAP version):
  2. One workaround by removing MARA via CL_OSQL_REPLACE=>GET_ACTIVE_REPLACEMENTS and CL_OSQL_REPLACE=>ACTIVATE_REPLACEMENT between CDS and OSQL environment creations (code here: https://community.sap.com/khhcw49343/attachments/khhcw49343/application-developmentforum-board/2020507/2/reproducer.txt)

@burghard and others, any idea?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: BSP component view rendering issue while launching fr...
entry.content:
Hello Vinod,

If I translate your requirement technically, it is like you want to launch a Stateful call encapsulated within a Stateless call.

BSP = Stateful --> Where Session should persist till the life cycle of launched bsp page.

Fiori = Stateless --> Session detached with persistency.

Technically, it will not be feasible for the given requirement. If somehow, we launch BSP from fiori tile, it will not fit for further navigation and event handling. As soon you click Fiori tile, session of inner section (BSP) will be disconnected from server and any next event rendering should not be responsive.

I can only of possible way treating fiori tile as transaction launcher of type URL. This should be opened as new window which is as good as opening the BSP application (Web UI).

Please consider creating Fiori app extension or developing the new app depending upon requirement and handle via OData as RESTful.

Regards,

Rajeev Singh

entry.title: Re: What settings do I need to make on the materail maste...
entry.content:
Hello @PlamenAKirilov ,

I am not MM expert but as far as I know, you do not need special material settings beyond allowing decimal quantities for the unit of measure (meters). The standard process is to issue the full quantity to the order using movement type 261 and later return the unused remainder in partial quantities using movement type 262. SAP fully supports multiple partial returns such as 1m, 2.3m, etc., as long as decimal quantities are enabled for the material’s unit of measure.

 

Requirement / ScenarioRecommended SAP MM Setup
Business scenarioYou issue a full quantity to an order (e.g. 100 meters), but later return the unused remainder in smaller pieces (1m, 2.3m, 5m, etc.).
Standard SAP capability✔ This is standard SAP behavior and does not require special material type.
Important material settingMaterial should use a unit of measure allowing decimals (e.g. M = meters with decimal quantities enabled).
Key configurationIn material master: ✔ Base Unit of Measure = M (meters) ✔ Decimal quantities allowed
Goods Issue processIssue full quantity to order using: ✔ MIGO or movement type 261
Return unused quantityReturn remaining partial quantities using: ✔ Movement type 262 (reversal of 261)
ExampleGI to order = 100m Actual consumption = 91.7m Return via 262 = 8.3m
Can returns happen in multiple pieces?✔ Yes. You can post multiple partial returns (1m, 2.3m, etc.) as long as total does not exceed issued quantity.
Material valuation impactSAP adjusts stock and order consumption automatically according to issued vs returned quantity.
Batch/serial requirement❌ Not mandatory unless business specifically requires traceability.
Recommended additional setupMaintain proper decimal precision in: ✔ CUNI (Unit of Measure settings)
Common issueIf decimals are not allowed for the UoM, SAP rounds quantities and partial meter returns become impossible.
Important movement types✔ 261 = GI to order ✔ 262 = Return/reversal from order


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: [Bug] in method cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds
entry.content:
@burghard In ABAP 7.58 SP 1, I reproduce exactly the problems described and the SAP code is the same as described in the original question, BUT I don't agree with the analysis or it works differently in ABAP 7.58 SP 1:

  1. Two of the three test methods of the code attached still succeed as originally said, but they fail when using INSERT_TEST_DATA: "The test double 'I_PRODUCT' not found"", CX_CDS_FAILURE=>TEST_DOUBLE_NOT_FOUND in the method IF_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT~GET_DOUBLE of CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT.
    I post below the code of INSERT_TEST_DATA.
  2. The way the test doubles are used is incorrect, or it works differently in ABAP 7.58 SP 1.
    I propose below two scenarios how to use the CDS and OSQL test doubles.

How to use the CDS and OSQL test doubles:

  1. If I_PRODUCT and MARA are to be mocked independently, which make it possibly incoherent (querying I_PRODUCT returning one product A and querying MARA returning one product B for instance), there's no need to use CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT, instead use CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT:
cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'I_PRODUCT' )
                                                               ( 'MARA' ) ) ).
  1. ---
  2. If mock data is to be injected in MARA and that should impact the CDS views based on MARA (I_PRODUCT), use only CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT, not CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT, and enable the double redirection in order to make SELECT FROM MARA read the data:
m_cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds(
              i_for_entities = VALUE #( ( i_for_entity = 'I_PRODUCT'
                                          i_select_base_dependencies = abap_true ) ) ).

" Explicit enabling of double redirection, although CREATE_FOR_MULTIPLE_CDS implicitly enables it.
m_cds_test_environment->enable_double_redirection( ).

 

Appendix

Full code, with test classes LCL_TEST_* from the original question + implementations of TEST methods by me + new test classes LTCL_MOCK_* which implement the 2 solutions above:

**********************************************************************
*
* The classes LCL_TEST_* are copied from this question (1st link), specifically this attachment (2nd link),
* but I have added an implementation to the method TEST to show that INSERT_TEST_DATA fails anyway:
* https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-discussions/bug-in-method-cl-cds-test-environment-gt-create-for-multiple-cds/td-p/12732251
* https://community.sap.com/khhcw49343/attachments/khhcw49343/application-developmentforum-board/2020507/2/reproducer.txt)
*
* The classes LTCL_MOCK_* are added by me, they work as desired in ABAP 7.58 SP 1.
*
**********************************************************************

**********************************************************************
* Classes LCL_TEST_*
**********************************************************************

** This is a test class to reproduce the error
** How to use:
** 1. Create a new class in the local package (you can give it any name)
** 2. Open the Test Classes section
** 3. Copy the code below
** 4. Execute unit tests.

** Expected result: all 3 tests should be green
** Actual result: One test is red.



*"* use this source file for your ABAP unit test classes


CLASS lcl_test_single DEFINITION FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: test FOR TESTING.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA m_cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment .
    CLASS-DATA m_osql_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment .
    CLASS-METHODS: class_setup.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_test_multi DEFINITION FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: test FOR TESTING.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA m_cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment .
    CLASS-DATA m_osql_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment .
    CLASS-METHODS: class_setup.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_test_multi_with_fix DEFINITION FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS: test FOR TESTING.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-DATA m_cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment .
    CLASS-DATA m_osql_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment .
    CLASS-METHODS: class_setup.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_test_single IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD test.
    TYPES ty_products TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_product WITH EMPTY KEY.

    DATA(injected_products) = VALUE ty_products( ( product = 'A' ) ).
    " INSERT_TEST_DATA fails "The test double 'I_PRODUCT' not found"
    " (CX_CDS_FAILURE=>TEST_DOUBLE_NOT_FOUND in the method IF_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT~GET_DOUBLE of CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT).
    m_cds_test_environment->insert_test_data( i_data = injected_products ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD class_setup.
    " this setup shows that creating a double for the CDS view I_PRODUCT, followed by creating a double for the MARA table is possible
    " when the (single) create method of cl_cds_test_environment is used.
    "
    m_cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create(  i_for_entity  = 'I_PRODUCT' ).
    m_osql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'MARA' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.



CLASS lcl_test_multi IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD test.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD class_setup.
    m_cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds( i_for_entities = VALUE #( ( i_for_entity = 'I_PRODUCT' ) ) ).

    " CREATE below fails "Double/Clone cannot be created. Double/Clone already exists for 'MARA'"
    " (CX_OSQL_FAILURE=>DOUBLE_EXIST in the method VALIDATE_DOUBLES of CL_OSQL_TEST_ENVIRONMENT).
    m_osql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'MARA' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.




CLASS lcl_test_multi_with_fix IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD test.
    TYPES ty_products TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_product WITH EMPTY KEY.

    DATA(injected_products) = VALUE ty_products( ( product = 'A' ) ).
    " INSERT_TEST_DATA fails "The test double 'I_PRODUCT' not found"
    " (CX_CDS_FAILURE=>TEST_DOUBLE_NOT_FOUND in the method IF_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT~GET_DOUBLE of CL_CDS_TEST_ENVIRONMENT).
    m_cds_test_environment->insert_test_data( i_data = injected_products ).
  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD class_setup.
    m_cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds( i_for_entities = VALUE #( ( i_for_entity = 'I_PRODUCT' ) ) ).

    " the call to create_for_multiple_cds erroneously creates entries for MARFA and MAW1
    " in the cl_osql_replace replacement list.
    " We can work around this bug by removing the spurious entries as follows:
    DATA(lt_replacements) = cl_osql_replace=>get_active_replacements( ).

    LOOP AT lt_replacements INTO DATA(ls_replacement).
      IF ls_replacement-source = 'MARA' OR ls_replacement-source = 'MAW1'.
        DELETE lt_replacements.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
    TRY.
        cl_osql_replace=>activate_replacement( replacement_table = lt_replacements ).
      CATCH cx_osql_replace INTO DATA(l_error).
        cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_bound( act = l_error ).
    ENDTRY.

    " after the cl_osql_replace replacement list has been cleaned up, a test double for MARA can be created.
    m_osql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'MARA' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.


**********************************************************************
* Classes LTCL_MOCK_*
**********************************************************************

CLASS ltcl_mock_cds_table_independen DEFINITION FINAL
  FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS test FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.

    CLASS-METHODS class_setup.

    CLASS-DATA m_osql_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_osql_test_environment.

    TYPES ty_products TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_product WITH EMPTY KEY.
    TYPES ty_mara     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF mara WITH EMPTY KEY.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_mock_cds_table_independen IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD class_setup.
    m_osql_test_environment = cl_osql_test_environment=>create( i_dependency_list = VALUE #( ( 'I_PRODUCT' )
                                                                                             ( 'MARA' ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test.
    DATA(injected_products) = VALUE ty_products( ( product = 'A' ) ).
    m_osql_test_environment->insert_test_data( injected_products ).
    DATA(injected_mara) = VALUE ty_mara( ( mandt = sy-mandt matnr = 'B' ) ).
    m_osql_test_environment->insert_test_data( injected_mara ).

    SELECT *
      FROM i_product
      INTO TABLE @DATA(actual_products).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = actual_products
                                        exp = injected_products ).
    SELECT *
      FROM mara
      INTO TABLE @DATA(actual_mara).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = actual_mara
                                        exp = injected_mara ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_mock_tables_only_multi DEFINITION FINAL
  FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS test FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.

    CLASS-METHODS class_setup.

    CLASS-DATA m_cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment.

    TYPES ty_products TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_product WITH EMPTY KEY.
    TYPES ty_mara     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF mara WITH EMPTY KEY.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_mock_tables_only_multi IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD class_setup.
    m_cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create_for_multiple_cds(
                                 i_for_entities = VALUE #( ( i_for_entity               = 'I_PRODUCT'
                                                             i_select_base_dependencies = abap_true ) ) ).
    " Explicit enabling of double redirection, although CREATE_FOR_MULTIPLE_CDS implicitly enables it.
    m_cds_test_environment->enable_double_redirection( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test.
    DATA(injected_mara) = VALUE ty_mara( ( mandt = sy-mandt matnr = 'A' ) ).
    m_cds_test_environment->insert_test_data( injected_mara ).

    SELECT *
      FROM i_product
      INTO TABLE @DATA(actual_products).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = actual_products
                                        exp = VALUE ty_products( ( product        = 'A'
                                                                   isactiveentity = abap_true ) ) ).
    SELECT *
      FROM mara
      INTO TABLE @DATA(actual_mara).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = actual_mara
                                        exp = injected_mara ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_mock_tables_only_single DEFINITION FINAL
  FOR TESTING RISK LEVEL HARMLESS DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS test FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.

    CLASS-METHODS class_setup.

    CLASS-DATA m_cds_test_environment TYPE REF TO if_cds_test_environment.

    TYPES ty_products TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i_product WITH EMPTY KEY.
    TYPES ty_mara     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF mara WITH EMPTY KEY.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_mock_tables_only_single IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD class_setup.
    m_cds_test_environment = cl_cds_test_environment=>create( i_for_entity               = 'I_PRODUCT'
                                                             i_select_base_dependencies = abap_true ).
    " Explicit enabling of double redirection, because CREATE doesn't enable it implicitly.
    m_cds_test_environment->enable_double_redirection( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test.
    DATA(injected_mara) = VALUE ty_mara( ( mandt = sy-mandt matnr = 'A' ) ).
    m_cds_test_environment->insert_test_data( injected_mara ).

    SELECT *
      FROM i_product
      INTO TABLE @DATA(actual_products).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = actual_products
                                        exp = VALUE ty_products( ( product        = 'A'
                                                                   isactiveentity = abap_true ) ) ).
    SELECT *
      FROM mara
      INTO TABLE @DATA(actual_mara).
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals( act = actual_mara
                                        exp = injected_mara ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: What do you wish someone would build for SAP + AI?
entry.content:
For anyone running SAP + AI in their org:

If you could redesign your SAP-AI architecture from scratch this weekend — with no SAP politics, no policy v4/2026, no keynote pressure — what would it actually look like?

Specifically:
1. Where would your data live? Inside SAP, in your warehouse, in a lake, somewhere else?
2. Who would you trust to make AI decisions for your team — SAP/Joule, Anthropic, OpenAI, in-house, multi-vendor?
3. What would "compliant by default" mean for your CIO and audit team? Is it logging? Data residency? A specific architecture pattern?
4. What's the thing you wish existed that nobody's built? The "if only there were a tool that..." sentence?

I keep reading articles about what SAP did. I want to know what customers actually want instead.

Not building anything specific yet. Mapping wishes before committing to a direction.

Comment, DM, or just reply with one of the four. Even partial answers useful.

entry.title: Anyone else using AI to help build SAP B1 reports?
entry.content:
Curious to hear how others approach reporting work in SAP B1 — especially in multi-entity setups on HANA.

In my day-to-day I get a lot of management requests that turn into multi-table cross-entity queries, often with data quality issues to clean up along the way. I've been experimenting with using Claude/ChatGPT to help refine the trickier joins and HANA-specific syntax, plus a personal checklist for validating data before reports go out.

A few things I'm curious about:

  1. Are others using AI for SAP B1 SQL work? What's working, what isn't?
  2. What kinds of reports do you find most painful or time-consuming to build?
  3. How do you handle data-entry inconsistencies that affect report outputs?

Just trying to learn from how others approach this. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: create identity column
entry.content:
'int' and 'integer' are interchangeable as datatype specifiers, however, 'identity' appears to only allow 'int'.   I will check on that.

For the second issue: you cannot supply values to an identity column unless you first explicitly set 'identity_insert <tablename> on' for that table as documented in Explicitly Insert Data into an IDENTITY Column .

Chris

entry.title: Re: UK Postponed Import VAT Accounting in SAP
entry.content:
Hello @VanessaBrixle ,

The standard SAP best practice for UK Postponed Import VAT (PIVA) is usually not to create a supplier invoice, but instead to use a dedicated reverse-charge/tax-only VAT process that creates simultaneous input and output VAT postings without affecting supplier balances or net expenses. Most UK SAP implementations configure dedicated PIVA tax codes and post the monthly HMRC statement via FI journal entry (e.g. FB50) so the VAT return updates correctly while avoiding supplier liability postings.

 

Question / RequirementSAP Best Practice & Explanation
What is the business requirement?HMRC PIVA requires companies to account for import VAT through the VAT return without physically paying VAT at customs. This creates simultaneous: ✔ Output VAT ✔ Input VAT
Desired accounting effect✔ Debit Input VAT ✔ Credit Output VAT ✔ No supplier balance impact ✔ No inventory/expense impact
Standard SAP approach used by UK companiesMost companies use: ✔ Tax-only journal entry ✔ Reverse charge VAT logic ✔ Manual FI document with dedicated tax code
Is supplier invoice recommended?⚠ Usually NOT ideal for monthly HMRC PIVA statements because there is no true supplier liability.
Recommended SAP solution✔ Create dedicated PIVA tax code configured for reverse-charge / tax-only posting behavior.
Typical posting entryExample: Dr Input VAT G/L £10,000 Cr Output VAT G/L £10,000
How posting is usually doneVia: ✔ FB50 (GL posting) ✔ or tax-only journal process
Why your VF tax code approach errorsSAP tax configuration likely does not currently allow: ✔ Multiple tax line assignments ✔ Tax-only postings ✔ Reverse charge logic
Important configuration areas✔ Tax procedure ✔ OB40 tax account determination ✔ Tax code settings ✔ Non-deductible tax indicators
Common SAP design for PIVAConfigure dedicated tax codes specifically for: ✔ Import VAT self-accounting ✔ Reverse charge VAT ✔ VAT-only postings
Does SAP support this standardly?✔ Yes, but usually through reverse-charge/tax-only configuration rather than standard supplier invoice process.
Recommended posting modelUse GL-based tax-only posting rather than AP invoice if there is no actual supplier payable.
VAT return impactCorrectly configured tax code updates both: ✔ Box 1 (Output VAT) ✔ Box 4 (Input VAT)
Important UK localization pointMany UK SAP implementations configure dedicated PIVA tax codes after Brexit/HMRC PIVA introduction.
Best practiceKeep PIVA postings separated from actual supplier invoices and customs duty postings.


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Difference of advanced foreign currency valuation and...
entry.content:
Hello @FabianSCC ,

In classical foreign currency valuation, SAP allows separate exchange rate types for debit and credit balances. However, in Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation (AFCV), this standard functionality is not available in the same way. AFCV uses a simplified valuation framework in S/4HANA and generally supports one exchange rate determination logic per valuation method rather than separate debit/credit exchange rate types. If the customer has a strict requirement for different debit and credit exchange rate handling, you may need to evaluate workaround designs such as separate valuation methods or enhancement logic.

 

QuestionExplanation
In classical foreign currency valuation, can different exchange rate types be maintained for debit and credit balances?✔ Yes. In classical valuation, SAP allows separate exchange rate types for: ✔ Debit balances ✔ Credit balances
Your screenshotThe highlighted fields show this standard classical valuation capability using separate exchange rate types (e.g. M/M or different types).
Is the same possible in Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation (AFCV)?❌ No, not in the same way. Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation does not provide standard configuration to maintain separate exchange rate types independently for debit and credit balances within one valuation method.
Why this difference existsAFCV is based on the newer S/4HANA valuation framework and simplified valuation logic/universal journal architecture.
Standard AFCV behaviorAFCV generally uses: ✔ One valuation principle/method ✔ One exchange rate logic per valuation area/method
Customer requirement impactIf customer requires different debit/credit exchange rate types exactly like classical valuation, this can become a functional gap in AFCV.
Possible workaroundPotential options: ✔ Separate valuation methods ✔ Separate account grouping ✔ BAdI/enhancement logic (if feasible)
Important considerationSAP intentionally simplified parts of FX valuation logic in AFCV to align with Universal Parallel Accounting/S4 architecture.
RecommendationValidate whether the business requirement is truly regulatory/accounting-driven or simply historical ECC behavior.
Migration considerationCustomers moving from classical valuation to AFCV often encounter differences in: ✔ valuation logic ✔ posting behavior ✔ exchange rate determination


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: t code to extract SO released in a particular day
entry.content:
Hello @DebS2026 ,

The best way to identify the number of sales orders released per day by user is typically through SAP change documents rather than standard sales order tables alone. You should analyze release-related status changes using tables CDHDR and CDPOS together with VBAK/VBUK, where CDHDR provides the user and timestamp information. The exact logic depends on what “released” means in your process (credit release, approval, delivery block removal, etc.), but this is the standard and most reliable approach for generating volume metrics by day and user.

 

RequirementRecommended SAP Approach
Need volume metric of sales orders released per dayYou need to track: ✔ Sales Order release date/time ✔ User who released it ✔ Company code / sales org filtering
Standard SAP challengeSAP does not always store a dedicated “released by” field directly in VBAK. Often this must be derived from: ✔ Change documents ✔ Status changes ✔ Workflow logs
Best standard sourceUse change documents tables: ✔ CDHDR (header) ✔ CDPOS (field changes)
What to trackIdentify the field/status that changes when order is “released” (credit release, delivery block removal, approval status, etc.).
Useful sales order tables✔ VBAK – Sales order header ✔ VBUK – Overall processing status ✔ VBAP – Item data
User identificationCDHDR-USERNAME gives the SAP user who performed the release/change.
Date trackingCDHDR-UDATE + UTIME provide release date/time.
Volume metric outputYou can aggregate by: ✔ Day ✔ User ✔ Company code / sales org
Important clarification“Released” can mean different things in SAP: ✔ Credit release ✔ Workflow approval ✔ Delivery block removal ✔ ATP release
If credit release scenarioUse tables/functions related to: ✔ VKM* transactions ✔ Credit status changes
Reporting approachRecommended options: ✔ SQVI/QuickViewer ✔ CDS View ✔ BW/SAC report ✔ Custom ABAP query
Fastest practical solutionBuild report joining: VBAK ↔ CDHDR ↔ CDPOS
Example logicCount distinct sales orders where release-related field changed from blocked → released grouped by user/date
Company code considerationSales orders are usually tied more directly to: ✔ Sales Organization ✔ Company code derivation via sales area
Recommended fields✔ VBAK-VBELN ✔ VBAK-VKORG ✔ CDHDR-USERNAME ✔ CDHDR-UDATE

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite - iSeries (AS400) DB2 JDBC connection
entry.content:
1. Establish a connection between a MSSQL Server instance and IBMi via Linked Server
If you can query data on your MSSQL Server from your IBMi tables/views go on with:
2. In your Cloud Connector instance connected to your Integration Suite subaccount add a mapping to your MSSQL Server instance
Backend Type: Non-SAP System
Protocol: TCP 
Virtual Host: any host Name
Virtual Port: any port number
Internal Host: your MSSQL Server host Name or IP address
Internal Port: your MSSQL Server port
3. In CPI Monitor Section under JDBC Material add a JDBC Driver for MSSQL
Microsoft JDBC Driver for SQL Server Version: 8.4.1
4. In CPI Monitor Section under JDBC Material add a JDBC Data Source for your MSSQL Server instance
Database Type: Microsoft SQL Server (On-Premise)
User: any User configured on your MSSQL Server
JDBC URL: jdbc:sqlserver://{Virtual Host from CC}:{Virtual Port from CC};instanceName={your MSSQL instance};DatabaseName={your MSSQL Database}
Cloud Connector: true
Location ID: {from CC-Configuration}
5. Create an iFlow with Request Reply Step using JDBC-Adapter
JDBC Data Source Alias: {your JDBC Data Source Name from 4.}
6. As input payload the Request Reply Step needs the SQL-Statement to be sent to your MSSQL Server
This can be prepared in a Content Modifier Step before Request Reply
7. Take the XML-Response and map and send it to whatever you want

entry.title: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content:
Hi All!

We are currently converting our ECC NW750 EHP8 systems to S/4HANA 2023. During testing an issue was recently discovered with long texts (STXH/STXL) where TDNAME is a concatenation of material number (MATNR), sales org (VKORG) and distribution channel (VTWEG). The issue is affecting our custom text IDs (e.g. Z001 or Z005) for standard object MVKE. These long texts are maintained via a custom tab in material maintenance and existing texts are no longer displayed in systems converted to S/4HANA.

Comparable standard text IDs (e.g. 0001) don't have this issue and the long texts are displayed in MM03 regardless of system version. Taking a look at the data in STXH, the differences are easy to see: for the standard texts in the S/4HANA system, TDNAME has 22 space characters between the 18 characters needed for the MATNR (we are not switching to 40 chars) and the sales org + distribution channel. For the comparable custom text, these "filler characters" are missing.

Random example long texts showing issue with TDNAME for custom long texts

If we manually add our custom text to a MATNR in S/4HANA, TDNAME includes the filler spaces. So, we have an issue with all impacted customer long texts created in NW750 before the system conversion but not the ones created in S/4HANA.

I already found a short mention about the issue in generic simplification note "2215871 - Material Number Field Length Extension - Data Migration"

Long Texts

Tables STXH and STXL are converted to the described rules for concatenated fields. The corresponding conversion program will do the conversion based on the type of the long text for SAP delivered application object and text ids. Custom application objects or custom text ids on SAP application objects may need to be converted like described below for custom tables.

Unfortunately, there is no actual description "below" in the note for custom text ids from what I can tell.

What I couldn't find is the actual name of the mentioned "conversion program". I'd however really like to see how this conversion happens for the standard long texts in the hope to be able to get some clues of how we can do something similar for our custom long texts.

I know that we could theoretically "copy" the impacted long texts via a combination of READ_TEXT and CREATE_TEXT with adapting the TDNAME inbetween. This will however create new long texts with current dates and user information and not retain the original information, so isn't really a good option. 

I also already identified the Z-code where our custom long text for MVKE/Z001 gets "build" within MM0n, but that code only calls function module LANGTEXT_INIT and passes the material number as a parameter typed as MATNR. So, there's no option to interfere with how TDNAME gets build before e.g. READ_TEXT (or similar) is called in SAP standard code.

  CALL FUNCTION 'LANGTEXT_INIT'
       EXPORTING
            P_MATNR          = RMMG1-MATNR
            P_VKORG          = RMMG1-VKORG
            P_VTWEG          = RMMG1-VTWEG
            P_AKTYP          = T130M-AKTYP
            P_TDID          = Z_TEXT_ID3              "KRM01
            LANGTEXTBILD     = LANGTEXTBILD
            P_KZPROZ         = KZ_VERT_PROZ
            P_REF_MATNR      = RMMG1_REF-MATNR
            P_REF_VKORG     = RMMG1_REF-VKORG
            P_REF_VTWEG      = RMMG1_REF-VTWEG
            FLGNUMINT        = FLGNUMINT
            NEUFLAG          = NEUFLAG
            RMMG2_FLG_RETAIL = RMMG2-FLG_RETAIL  "mk/1.2B
             IMPORTING
            P_KZPROZ         = KZ_VERT_PROZ
            P_KZ_LANGTEXT    = RMMZU-MG_LANGTEX.

Here are my two questions:

  1. Has anybody run into this issue during a system conversion from ECC to S/4HANA and if yes, how did you tackle it?
  2. Does anybody know the name of the conversion program apparently used during system conversion to "update" TDNAME of impacted standard long texts?

Thanks much for any suggestions you have!

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Working Smarter with Date Functions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and BTP ABAP Environment (Steampunk)
entry.content:
Introduction

If you've ever hardcoded a date in a SELECT statement or asked a user to manually key in a date range every time they run a report, you'll appreciate what Date Functions bring to the table.

Date Functions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and the SAP BTP ABAP Environment (Steampunk) let you define dynamic, human-readable date definitions — think FirstDayOfCurrentMonth or LastDayOfCurrentYear or CURRENT_QUARTER  or Next30DaysFromToday orTOMORROW, CURRENT_QUARTER, or LAST_FISCAL_YEAR — that automatically resolve to the correct calendar values at runtime. No hardcoding. No manual input. Just clean, reusable date logic that works across analytical queries, ABAP code, and CDS views.

In this post, I'll walk through what Date Functions are, who uses them, and how developers and key users can leverage them across the stack.

What Are Date Functions?

A Date Function is a named, dynamic date or date range definition. Instead of storing a fixed date, you define a rule — and the system calculates the actual value at runtime based on the current system date.

 

Supported Combinations

SAP supports three types of Date Function definitions:

  • Single Date — A dynamic single-date definition. For example, a Date Function defined as TOMORROW will always return the date for tomorrow. If today is 21st May 2026, the function returns 22nd May 2026 — automatically, every time.
  • Relative to Base Date Range — A date range defined with an offset (days, weeks, months, quarters, or years) relative to a single dynamic base date.
  • Configured Range — A date range with both a dynamic start date and a dynamic end date, giving full flexibility for complex time window definitions.

SAP has delivered more than 40 predefined Date Functions out of the box, and the Manage Date Functions app has been available to key users since 2017.

The Three Personas — Who Uses Date Functions and How

Date Functions are designed to serve three distinct personas, each interacting with them differently.

1. Key User — Define and Manage

The Key User is the starting point. Using the Manage Date Functions application, key users can:

  • Browse and explore SAP's 40+ predefined Date Functions
  • Create custom Date Functions tailored to their business calendar or reporting needs
  • Simulate the dynamic values a Date Function will return, before it goes live
  • Transport Date Functions from development through to quality and production systems

This is a no-code capability — no developer involvement needed to define or adjust Date Functions. It puts time-intelligence directly in the hands of functional consultants and power users.

 

2. Developer — Use in CDS Views

The Developer persona integrates Date Functions directly into CDS view source code.

A developer can declare a Date Function parameter in a CDS view with a default value. This makes the CDS view inherently time-aware — the date range it operates on is no longer hardcoded but driven by a named, maintainable definition.

Key users can then further customise Date Function parameters through apps like Custom Analytical Queries, without touching the underlying code.

 

Coming soon: SAP is also working on enabling developers to define Date Functions directly within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) — using the same development environment as CDS views, with a form-based UI and simulate/test capabilities built in. This is currently in progress (WIP) and subject to change, but it will further unify the developer experience.

3. Business User — Consume in Analytical Reports

The Business User benefits from Date Functions through Analytical Reporting tools built on Date-Function-enabled CDS views.

Instead of entering raw date values, users:

  • Select from a value help showing all available Date Functions with their human-readable names
  • Easily toggle between different Date Functions to shift the analysis window — for example, switching from CURRENT_MONTH to PREVIOUS_MONTH with a single selection
  • Get results that are always current and accurate, because the date range resolves at query execution time

This dramatically simplifies the reporting experience and reduces the chance of user error when specifying time periods.

 

4. ABAP Developer — Consume via API

For developers who need to use dynamic date logic in ABAP programs, reports, or classes, Date Functions are accessible via a C1-released CDS view API.

This API returns the resolved dynamic date or date range for a given Date Function — which the developer can then use directly in SELECT statements or any time-based processing logic.

 
 
" Example: Use the Date Function API to resolve a dynamic date range
" and filter records within that time frame
SELECT * FROM your_cds_view
  WHERE posting_date BETWEEN @LV_start_date AND @LV_end_date.

The value help integration also works in ABAP contexts, making it easy to discover and reference available Date Functions programmatically.

 

Coming soon: SAP is also working on enabling developers to define Date Functions directly within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) — using the same development environment as CDS views, with a form-based UI and simulate/test capabilities built in. This is currently in progress (WIP) and subject to change, but it will further unify the developer experience.

Why This Matters for Steampunk / BTP ABAP Cloud Environment

If you're building on the SAP BTP ABAP Environment (Steampunk), Date Functions are particularly valuable because:

  • They align with the clean core philosophy — time logic lives in a maintainable, transportable definition rather than scattered across code
  • They support extensibility without modification — key users can evolve date definitions independently of developer cycles
  • The CDS parameter approach keeps analytical views flexible and reusable across multiple time horizons
  • The upcoming ADT integration will make Date Functions a first-class citizen of the developer toolchain, consistent with how other CDS artefacts are managed

Summary

Persona Tool Capability
Key UserManage Date Functions appDefine, simulate, and transport Date Functions
DeveloperCDS ViewsDeclare Date Function parameters with defaults
Business UserAnalytical ReportsSelect Date Functions via value help at runtime
ABAP DeveloperABAP Development Tool Kit (ADT)Resolve dynamic dates for use in ABAP logic (WIP)

 

Coming soon: SAP is also working on enabling developers to define Date Functions directly within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) — using the same development environment as CDS views, with a form-based UI and simulate/test capabilities built in. This is currently in progress (WIP) and subject to change, but it will further unify the developer experience.

ABAP Developer Define and Consume Custom Date Function in ADT (Development User)

High-Impact Use Cases

  • Dynamic Financial Period Reporting: Instead of hard-coding dates, developers can use the PREVIOUSMONTH or Fiscal Year conversion reuse functions. This allows an analytical dashboard to automatically adjust its data range based on the system's current date without any code changes.
  • SLA and Deadline Tracking: Partners can leverage standardized functions like DATN_DAYS_BETWEEN or utcl_add_seconds to calculate service level agreement (SLA) breaches or invoice due dates in real-time. This ensures that "Time to Resolve" calculations are identical whether they run on-stack in S/4HANA or side-by-side on BTP.
  • Time-Zone Aware Global Operations: For apps serving users in different regions, reuse components like abap_system_timezone or utcl_to_local automatically handle daylight savings and regional conversions. This is critical for logistics or workforce management apps where a "Requested Delivery Date" must be accurately localized for warehouses worldwide.

Key Benefits of Adoption

  • Zero-Downtime Upgrades: Because these components are "Released APIs" (C1 contract), they are guaranteed to remain stable during automated SAP cloud upgrades.
  • Performance Optimization: Date Functions is significantly faster than fetching data into the application layer for manual date math.
  • Reduced Development Maintenance: By using Date Functions partners offload the maintenance.

 

 👉 Please let us know if you have any busines case / functional usecase in which you would like to use the Custom Date Function in ABAP Development Tool Kit (ADT) 

👉 Drop your experience or questions in the comments — always happy to discuss!

👉reach out to murthy.v@sap.com achim.clemens@sap.com nitin.padmanabhan@sap.com ashwin.k.s@sap.com 

👉Have you used Date Functions in your projects? Drop your experience or questions in the comments — always happy to discuss!

entry.title: From Concept to action: Hands-On with the CAP Operator and the Partner Reference Application
entry.content:
Introduction

In my recent blog series, Kyma Evolution: Transforming SAP Kyma into a Tailor-Made SaaS Platform for CAP, I explored the architecture behind turning SAP BTP, Kyma runtime into a native, clean-core-compliant foundation for multitenant Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) applications. We discussed the concept of shifting heavy operational workloads—like tenant provisioning, container routing, and database migration—away from custom application logic and directly into the Kubernetes control plane via the CAP Operator.

Concepts and architecture diagrams are crucial, but as developers, we truly understand a framework when we see it working in real code.

To bridge the gap between architectural theory and practical deployment, I have created a hands-on technical walkthrough. I took the standard SAP-provided Partner Reference Application (PRA)—the gold standard sample for complex BTP multitenant SaaS engineering—and forked it to demonstrate exactly how to transition a standard CAP application into a declarative, CAP Operator-managed deployment.

You can find the complete implementation and step-by-step repository here: 👉ElectronicWizzard/partner-reference-application (kymapra1 branch) 

Why the Partner Reference Application?

The Partner Reference Application is designed to showcase enterprise-grade SaaS development on SAP BTP. It contains complex real-world challenges:

  • Multitenancy with isolated data access.
  • Integration with BTP core services (XSUAA, Destination, SaaS Provisioning Service).
  • Explicit lifecycle tasks for tenant onboarding and offboarding.

By migrating this specific application to the CAP Operator, we demonstrate that the operator isn't just for simple "Hello World" microservices. It is fully capable of managing sophisticated, production-grade enterprise architectures.

Here is a glimpse of the migration workflow detailed in the hands-on guide:

1. Project Preparation & Plugin Integration

Instead of writing complex Kubernetes deployment manifests from scratch, we leverage the tooling built into the CAP ecosystem. By injecting the development dependency into the project root:

npm add @cap-js/cap-operator-plugin -D

We unlock the plugin capabilities that assist in automatically deriving the necessary configuration files and container patterns directly from our existing cds models and configuration schema.

2. Containerization and Registry Push

As container-native deployments are standard practice, the workflow guides you through leveraging Docker to containerize the CAP application services and push them to a container registry accessible by your Kyma cluster.

3. Transitioning to Custom Resources (CRDs)

The heart of the transformation lies in replacing standard Kubernetes Deployments and StatefulSets with custom resources defined by the CAP Operator, such as CAPApplication.

Instead of writing custom logic to handle mtxs (multitenancy/extensibility) operations during tenant registration, you simply declare the application's characteristics in a clean, unified manifest. The CAP Operator watches this resource and automatically sets up:

  • The necessary application pods.
  • Automated routing rules.
  • Secure BTP Services integration using the SAP BTP Service Operator.
  • Automated tenant provisioning
  • ...

Prerequisites to Get Started

To follow along with the tutorial and deploy the project yourself, make sure you have the following environments prepared:

  1. A Kyma Cluster with the CAP Operator Installed: Follow the setup detailed in the Kyma Evolution series to ensure your cluster control plane is ready to interpret the custom CAP resources.

  2. BTP Provider Subaccount: A dedicated provider subaccount mapped to your Kyma namespace and entitlement of the required services.

  3. Local Tooling: Docker installed on your machine and access to a container registry (e.g., Docker Hub, GitHub Packages).

Conclusion & Next Steps

Shifting from imperative scripting to a declarative, operator-driven cloud runtime is a massive step forward for building resilient, clean-core SaaS applications on SAP BTP. By analyzing the changes in this fork, you will see exactly how much boilerplate code and deployment complexity can be stripped away from your project lifecycle.

Head over to the repository, check out the kymapra1 branch, and walk through the implementation: 🔗 Read the Full Deployment Guide on GitHub

For those who want to start with there own workload I would like to recommend the new SAP Tutorial for the CAP Operator on the Kyma Environment.

I would love to hear your thoughts! Have you started experimenting with the CAP Operator in your own Kyma or Kubernetes environments? What challenges or successes have you encountered during the shift to declarative operations? Let's discuss in the comments below.

entry.title: Business Partner @ SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public & Private Edition - Data Load Preparation
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome back to my Business Partner @ SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition series. We’re still in the Explore phase of the SAP Activate methodology—moving from workshop insights to concrete preparation for what comes next.

Next stop is Data Migration Preparation. Data migration is the process of taking existing data from a legacy system and preparing it for use in new SAP system

For more detailed guidance, head to SAP Activate in the Road Map Viewer – SAP for Me.
Navigation: Content > Explore > Data Load Preparation
This step is relevant not only for Public Cloud implementations but also for:

  • Lift & Shift / Shell Conversion
  • Greenfield approaches

 

Helpful Information

Recommended migration approach

Given the complexity of Business Partner, Customer, and Vendor master data structures, consider using Direct Transfer to pull data directly from the ECC source. This requires the DMIS add‑on. More details are available in SAP Note 3640423 – Release strategy and maintenance information for the ABAP add‑on DMIS 2025.

 

Data quality first

Before your initial migration cycles, run a consistency check and resolve issues in ECC. Execute transaction CVI_PRECHK (part of the CVI Cockpit; can be run standalone) to spot and clean up master data inconsistencies. For a practical walkthrough, see Soundar’s blog: CVI Cockpit – Master Data Consistency Check.

You’ll find end‑to‑end information on migration in the SAP Help Portal.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and On-Prem

In both migration scenarios (ECC without CVI and ECC with completed CVI), you can review the full list of supported objects in SAP Help Portal by navigating to 'Available Migration Objects'.

A welcome enhancement: starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (CE2602) and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (OP2025 FPS1), Direct Transfer supports Staging Tables as an intermediate storage layer. This lets you stage, validate, and control data loads more flexibly. For a practical walkthrough, see the SAP Community blog SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Directly from SAP System – Using Staging Tables.

 

Migration Scenarios

In this section, I’ll outline two common migration paths from an SAP ECC source system and how they influence the migration objects you’ll need.

  • ECC without active CVI: The system contains only customer and vendor master data with no linked Business Partners. Think of this as a “raw” starting point without transformation prep.
  • ECC with active CVI: The CVI conversion has already been completed as a pre‑project, so customers and vendors are synchronized with Business Partners.

 

ECC without active CVI

At this scenarion you did no changes to you master data in respect to customer and vendor. Meaning there are no Business Partners in the system. At following URLs you might adjust your target system release at help page.

Relevant Migration Objects are Customer and Vendor including Contact Persons for

On-Premise and Private Cloud.

  • Customer
    • Direct Transfer
    • Staging Tables
  • Supplier
    • Direct Transfer
    • Staging Tables

Cloud Public Edition

  • Customer
    • Direct Transfer
    • Staging Tables
  • Supplier
    • Direct Transfer
    • Staging Tables

With this scenario Business Partners are created automatically during migration in the target system.

 

ECC with active CVI

If your ECC source system has CVI active (CVI conversion completed), the supported approach is Direct Transfer using the Business Partner migration object. This single object covers Business Partners together with their linked customers, suppliers, and contact persons—aligning with the Cloud Public Edition principle that the Business Partner is the leading object and BP-to-customer/supplier counts align 1:1.

  • On-Premise and Private Cloud
  • Public Cloud

 

Migration with Staging Tables only

If you must run a staging-tables-only scenario, you should view the SAP S/4HANA Cloud data structures by downloading the template files and associating the structures with the data from the legacy system(s).

This approach is relevant if your source system is a non-SAP System.

By using “Migrate Data using Staging Tables” approach, you can download the migration objects templates to view the data structure. Moreover, by filling the staging tables with the files, you need to download templates to specify data and upload it back to your project. If filling staging tables using 3rd party tools, the template files can be used as a guide to the table structures and required fields.

A practical guide is available in the following SAP Community blog series:

  • Part 1: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit – Migrating data using staging tables and methods for populating the staging tables (transaction LTMC – deprecated with SAP S/4HANA 2021)
  • Part 2: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - Using SAP Data Services to load data to the staging tables
  • Part 3: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit - Using SAP HANA Smart Data Integration (SDI) to load data to the staging tables
  • Part 4: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - Using SAP HANA Studio to load data to the staging table

 

Special cases

Merge Customer and Supplier into a single Business Partner

If you need to combine roles so one Business Partner holds both Customer and Supplier data, follow the sequence that matches your starting point. The goal is to extend an already migrated record with the missing organizational levels.

Scenario A: Customer/Business Partner exists — add Supplier data

  • Step 1: Migrate Customer using the migration object “Customer.”
  • Step 2: Migrate Supplier data using the migration object “Customer – extend existing record by new org levels.”

Scenario B: Supplier/Business Partner exists — add Customer data

  • Step 1: Migrate Supplier using the migration object “Supplier.”
  • Step 2: Migrate Customer data using the migration object “Supplier – extend existing record by new org levels.”

Important
Extending an already migrated record is only possible with Staging Tables. In the Migrate Your Data app, navigate to Available Migration Objects to find the “extend existing record” variants and use the Staging Tables path.

 

Contract Accounting

If you want to migrate Contract Partner (Migration Object 'FI-CA - Contract partner'), you have to prepare Customer Business Partners via Mass Change adding Business Partner role MKK following these steps:

  1. Export Business Partner - using Fiori app F3251 - Export Master Data - Business Partners (MDC File Download)
    Select table BUT100 only
  2. Download and adjust Exported excel file by filling role MKK in related column
    Hint: add an 'I' for insert at column B (ACTION_CODE)
  3. Run mass change app using adjusted excel file - using Fiori app F1047A - Start Mass Maintenance - Business Partners (Consolidation and Mass Processing)

An example file für BUT100 rolle MKK update you can see below.

 

Conclusion

You’re set now to tackle Data Load Preparation with confidence. Whether you start from ECC without CVI or with CVI completed, choose the right path—Direct Transfer (with DMIS) and, where needed, Staging Tables—to streamline Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier migration. Clean up master data early with CVI_PRECHK and rely on Available Migration Objects plus SAP Help/Community guides to prevent surprises. From here, the focus shifts to executing your first migration cycle and validating results against your Explore-phase design.

Hi everyone,

Why I’m here

What you can expect from me

I’m looking forward to engaging with all of you and learning together!

Thanks for having me here,
Esther

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector, Tomcat related question
entry.content:
I am surprised that you did not get an answer to this question in a SAP support ticket. From my point of view, I don't see a reason why not?! Can you tell me the support ticket number?
But in a public forum like here, anyone can create an account. I don't know if you are an SAP customer, or a social engineer trying to gather some info for potential malicious attacks. I am not telling that you are, but that's the reason. It's a basic security principle not to disclose any information about the software, libraries and versions being used to arbitrary/anonymous users (need-to-know principle).

Regarding the relevance for an upgrade, the answer is that considering the security aspect, one should always upgrade Cloud Connector to its latest patch level. And besides, it is not supported that Cloud Connector's bundled Tomcat libraries are upgraded alone on your own. However, every new Cloud Connector version always comes with the latest available libraries that successfully passed all tests shortly before each release.

entry.title: Re: GL Role Restriction based on Company Code.
entry.content:
Hi  @ksiddiqui , 

As we can see on your screenshots you are trying to set restrictions to company code, however, the visibility is controlled by the restriction types labeled Read, Value Help / or only Read. Only the Write access is restricted on the "General" tab.

 

To do that you should set the restrictions on the access category to "restricted" to at least (write, read, value help) and (read, value help). 

Than on company code, you select the company codes you want to RESTRICT access.

 

On this demonstration case below (only for illustration), I set the restriction to company code 1010, so the user won't be able to see and write on this company code.

You can also can see that I've used the Leading Restrictions check box:

"That means that the value in this field is automatically inherited by other restriction types that also use that field."

 

You can find some more info here: How to Define Authorizations Based on Restrictions | SAP Help Portal

Also, you can check more detailed step by step info on the unit 5 of this Learning Path:

Performing Advanced Configuration and Role Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, EHS Envi…

Another tip: To understand which restrictions are granting access to certain values you can use the App Display Authorization Trace, detailed on unit 6 as well.

I hope this helps,

Kind Regards,

Raffaella
